title: Misik
content:

{{Quote|Keep on that one, I'll take these two.|Luke Skywalker, with regards to Misik and another scout|<ref name="RotJ" />}}
'''Misik''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as a [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout trooper]]. He was a deadly shot and helped develop Imperial [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] tactics.

As of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], he had been promoted to the rank of [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]], and was part of the Imperial detachment assigned to protect the second [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star's]] [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield]] [[Endor shield generator bunker|generator bunker]] situated on the [[Endor/Legends|Sanctuary Moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]]. Eventually, Misik found himself in pursuit of various [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] infiltrators on Endor and was killed when his speeder bike was damaged by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], causing it to crash into an overturned tree.

==Biography==
===Imperial career===
The [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="CVD" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]] Misik was the son of a [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]], and eventually became a member of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]], ultimately becoming a [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout trooper]]. Becoming a [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]]<ref name="Misikcard" /> by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> Misik worked closely with [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Barich]], a fellow scout trooper, in the development of [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] tactics.<ref name="Misikcard" />

Sometime by 4 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> Misik and Barich were selected to be a part of the Imperial detachment posted to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. Their mission was to defend the bunker that housed the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] protecting the second [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star]] during its construction.<ref name="RotJ" />

===Battle of Endor===
{{Quote|Luke on foot, trooper on bike… not good, not good.|Luke Skywalker, talking to himself during his confrontation with Misik|<ref name="NPR">[[Return of the Jedi: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''Return of the Jedi'': The National Public Radio Dramatization]]</ref>}}
[[File:MisikbattlingwithLukeSkywalker.png|thumb|right|270px|Misik battling Luke Skywalker]] Eventually, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] learned that a second Death Star was being constructed and dispatched a [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|strike team]], led by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], to the forest moon with the mission of destroying the shield bunker. It was the Alliance's hope that once the shield generator was sabotaged, a massed [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel Fleet]] could then approach and destroy the partially completed Death Star.<ref name="RotJ" />

During the Rebel infiltration, Misik and his fellow scout troopers stumbled across the Alliance soldiers in the woods surrounding the shield bunker. What ensued was a speeder bike chase between [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], Misik, Barich, and a number of other scout troopers. During the pursuit, Misik and another scout found themselves engaged with Skywalker; Misik's partner [[Drelosyn]] was eventually killed, and Misik and Skywalker locked their bikes together in an effort to make the other bailout or crash.<ref name="RotJ" />

[[File:MisikCrash.png|thumb|left|270px|Misik moments before his death]]
Realizing that they were on a collision course with a rapidly approaching tree, Misik broke off while Skywalker jumped from his doomed speeder bike. Hoping to take advantage of the situation, Misik turned his bike around in an attempt to gun down Skywalker with his speeder's blasters. However, Skywalker managed to deflect Misik's bolts with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and sliced the steering vane off Misik's bike as the scout trooper flew by. Powerless to save himself, Misik's speeder bike spun uncontrollably, eventually crashing into a nearby tree, [[Death/Legends|killing]] him.<ref name="RotJ" />

==Personality and traits==
Misik hoped for a promotion to the [[Emperor's Demonstration Team]].<ref name="Misikcard" />

==Skills and abiltiies==
Misik was a deadly shot.<ref name="Misikcard" />

==Equipment==
Misik wore Imperial [[Scout trooper armor/Legends|Scout trooper armor]], with a [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]]. He drove a [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|74-Z speeder bike]] and was armed with a [[Scout trooper blaster pistol/Legends|scout trooper blaster pistol]].<ref name="RotJ" />

==Behind the scenes==
Misik's first appearance was in the [[1983]] [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|novelization]] of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']], by [[James Kahn]], just prior to the film's release,<ref name="ROTJnovel">[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi'' novelization]]</ref> in which he is briefly seen during the famous speeder bike chase scene.<ref name="RotJ" /> As stuntmen [[Dirk Yohan Beer]],<ref name="Facebook">[[:File:Yohanbeer.png|Message from Yohan Beer]]</ref> [[Peter Diamond]],<ref name="DiamondAutograph">{{WebCite|url=http://www.starwarsautographcollecting.com/Autographs/StarWarsTrilogy/ANewHope/PeterDiamond.htm|text=Peter Diamond-Trilogy Autographs|work=starwarsautographcollecting.com|archivedate=05-10-2021|archiveurl=https://archive.vn/VMS9b}}</ref> and [[Frank Henson]]<ref name="PushedOffBike">{{Blogspot|subdomain=starwarsaficionado|url=2012/03/archive-blog-star-wars-stars-at.html|name=Star Wars Aficionado|text=Archive Blog: 'Star Wars' Stars At The Collectormania 2008 Event (Milton Keynes)}}</ref> all claimed the roles of the remaining three pursuing biker scouts, Misik was likely portrayed by stuntman [[Julius LeFlore]], who recalled filming scenes as a biker scout against a bluescreen.<ref name="ProcessOfElimination">{{Blogspot|subdomain=starwarsaficionado|url=2014/09/behind-scenes-just-another-day-at-skiff.html|name=Star Wars Aficionado|text=Behind The Scenes: Just Another Day At The Skiff!}}</ref> He was first identified as Misik on the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] card entitled "Corporal Misik."<ref name="Misikcard" />

Some events in the unabridged script for [[Return of the Jedi: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''The National Public Radio Dramatization'' of ''Return of the Jedi'']], released in [[1996]], conflict with those previously established in the film.<ref name="NPR" /> In the movie for ''Return of the Jedi'', Luke Skywalker's and Misik's speeder bikes entangle, causing Skywalker to jump from his bike before it crashes into an overturned tree stump.<ref name="RotJ" /> However, in the radio dramatization script, Skywalker manages to destroy Misik's partner's speeder, which causes Misik to disappear momentarily. However, he returns on the side of Skywalker, shooting at his speeder, and it is these actions of Misik which force Skywalker to abandon his speeder.<ref name="NPR" />

Furthermore, there is a contradiction between the film<ref name="RotJ" /> and the [[2003]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''.<ref name="RSIII">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''</ref> In the film, Luke Skywalker manages to strike Misik's speeder bike with his lightsaber with one shot;<ref name="RotJ" /> however in ''Rebel Strike'', Skywalker can miss his speeder bike, in which case Misik eventually comes around for another pass so Skywalker can hit him. This will repeat until Skywalker's slash is successful.<ref name="RSIII" />
